currency.name: Gold Imperial Crown
currency.description: The currency of the noble Houses. Large gold coins stamped with the draconic seal of the ruling Sovereign. Rarely seen in the Sump.
currency.flavor_text: Heavy. Cold. Worth more than a Blank's life.

currency.name: Silver Scale
currency.description: The merchant's coin. Thin silver pieces named for the draconic scale pattern pressed into each face.

currency.name: Copper Sump Farthing
currency.description: The coin of the people. Worn copper discs, often slick with canal residue. What the Blanks and laborers trade for bread, shelter, and silence.
currency.flavor_text: Greasy and green with age. Still spends.

simple_melee.name: Club
simple_melee.description: A heavy length of ironwood scavenged from the Sump canals, wrapped in oilcloth for grip. The weapon of choice for those who cannot afford steel.
simple_melee.flavor_text: Every Blank owns one. Most never need it. Most.

simple_melee.name: Dagger
simple_melee.description: A blade of black volcanic glass, edges sharper than steel but brittle. Ceremonial in Obsidian courts, deadly in the streets.
simple_melee.flavor_text: One good stab. Then it shatters. Make it count.

simple_melee.name: Greatclub
simple_melee.description: A repurposed construction maul — iron-banded oak, still flecked with mortar dust. Carried by laborers who found themselves in a war they didn't start.
simple_melee.flavor_text: The Citadel wall wasn't going to break itself.

simple_melee.name: Handaxe
simple_melee.description: A compact forging axe, its blade still bearing the faint orange shimmer of dragon-fire tempering. Balanced for throwing — a Pyre smith's sidearm.
simple_melee.flavor_text: Forged in dragon breath. Thrown in anger.

simple_melee.name: Javelin
simple_melee.description: A lightweight throwing spear with feathered fletching and a hollow shaft. Designed for Vortex aerial infantry — sails farther than it should.
simple_melee.flavor_text: From above, everything looks like a target.

simple_melee.name: Light Hammer
simple_melee.description: A smith's cross-peen hammer, shrunken for combat. Heavy head, short handle. Throws well when you've spent a lifetime swinging it.
simple_melee.flavor_text: The first weapon a Pyre apprentice ever holds.

simple_melee.name: Mace
simple_melee.description: A flanged mace of black iron, stamped with the Obsidian judicial seal. Used by Inquisitors to deliver the Church's final judgment. The flanges are designed to shatter Aether-infused bone.
simple_melee.flavor_text: The verdict is always the same.

simple_melee.name: Quarterstaff
simple_melee.description: Carved from the fossilized rib of a lesser dragon. Dense, warm to the touch, and resonant — mages favor it as a simple focus. The bone hums faintly when Aether is near.
simple_melee.flavor_text: The dragon died long ago. It still wants to be held.

simple_melee.name: Sickle
simple_melee.description: A curved farming sickle, its blade originally meant for wheat. The Stone-Blood clans sharpen them to a razor edge and pass them down through generations.
simple_melee.flavor_text: Reaps grain. Reaps men. The motion is the same.

simple_melee.name: Spear
simple_melee.description: Conscript standard-issue. Mass-forged in the Citadel armories for the Blank regiments — the expendable front line. Simple, sturdy, disposable. Like the men who carry them.
simple_melee.flavor_text: A sharp stick and a prayer. It's all they were given.

simple_ranged.name: Crossbow, Light
simple_ranged.description: A compact repeating crossbow of Vortex design. Uses a wind-channeled draw mechanism — lighter pull, faster reload. Favored by sky-scouts for its one-handed operation on the wing.
simple_ranged.flavor_text: Wind loads the string. You just aim.

simple_ranged.name: Dart
simple_ranged.description: A slender iron needle, balanced with a drop of lead at the tip. Sump assassins dip them in canal-scum poison. Cheap enough to lose. Deadly enough to kill a noble with the right placement.
simple_ranged.flavor_text: Doesn't look like much. That's the point.

simple_ranged.name: Shortbow
simple_ranged.description: A compact recurve bow of laminated horn and sinew. Standard hunting equipment for the Deep-Blood clans who stalk prey in the under-realms beyond the city walls.
simple_ranged.flavor_text: Game is scarce. Shots must count.

simple_ranged.name: Sling
simple_ranged.description: A leather sling — the weapon of Sump children, canal-workers, and those too poor even for a club. Ammo is free: there's always a loose stone in the Sump.

martial_melee.name: Battleaxe
martial_melee.description: A broad-bladed axe forged in the volcanic forges of House Ignis. The tempering process leaves a permanent red-orange sheen along the cutting edge. House Ignis warriors wield them with a distinctive two-handed overhead swing.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Fire never leaves the steel. It waits.

martial_melee.name: Flail
martial_melee.description: A spiked ball on a length of blessed chain. Wielded by Penitents as both weapon and self-flagellation tool. The links are engraved with prayers to the Sixth God — each strike is an act of worship.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Pain is prayer. Blood is offering.

martial_melee.name: Glaive
martial_melee.description: A polearm of Ferrum design, intended for defending battlements. The blade curves forward like a breaking wave — designed to hook and pull climbers from walls.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Ferrum walls don't fall. Ferrum blades make sure of it.

martial_melee.name: Greataxe
martial_melee.description: A massive executioner's axe forged in dragon-fire. The blade is blackened from extreme heat except for the edge, which glows faintly orange. House Pyre uses these for public executions of traitors to the Sovereign.
martial_melee.flavor_text: The crowd holds its breath. The blade doesn't care.

martial_melee.name: Greatsword
martial_melee.description: The finest greatsword in Solis-Grave. Forged from True Steel — an alloy of iron, carbon, and trace draconic essence that can only be smelted in dragon-fire. Reserved for Pyre champions and those who earn the House's favor through blood.
martial_melee.flavor_text: A blade that remembers the fire it was born in.

martial_melee.name: Halberd
martial_melee.description: A halberd with a scythe-like curved blade, designed to be swept through ranks of infantry. The socket is inscribed with Ignis fire-runes that glow when the wielder's blood runs hot.

martial_melee.name: Lance
martial_melee.description: A lightweight hollow lance used by Vortex sky-knights mounted on hippogriffs. Hollow channels whistle in the wind during diving charges — the sound alone has routed superstitious Blank regiments.
martial_melee.flavor_text: The screaming you hear is the lance. The dying comes next.

martial_melee.name: Longsword
martial_melee.description: The ceremonial longsword of Obsidian Archons — judge-magistrates who serve as both lawgivers and executioners. The obsidian-inlaid hilt carries a hidden compartment for a single drop of the condemned's blood.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Justice is sharp. Mercy is not included.

martial_melee.name: Maul
martial_melee.description: A brutal stone-headed maul wielded by the Stone-Blood clans. The head is carved from a single piece of mountain granite, bound to a petrified-wood haft with iron bands. No finesse. Pure impact.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Armor doesn't help. The bones break anyway.

martial_melee.name: Morningstar
martial_melee.description: A spiked morningstar of Tenebris design. The spikes are hollow — designed to inject the flesh-bound alchemical agents House Tenebris is infamous for. Each spike can hold a different payload.
martial_melee.flavor_text: First the puncture. Then the change.

martial_melee.name: Pike
martial_melee.description: A long infantry pike mass-produced in the Citadel foundries. Issued to Blank conscripts who are drilled to stand in formation and hold the line. The wood is untreated — it splinters. The men are untrained — they die.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Three rows deep. The first row knows they won't see sunset.

martial_melee.name: Rapier
martial_melee.description: A rapier with a blade of fused black volcanic glass. Impossible to sharpen by conventional means — the edge is formed at the molecular level during the glass-fusing process. Lighter than steel, deadlier than it looks.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Beauty kills. This blade is very, very beautiful.

martial_melee.name: Scimitar
martial_melee.description: A curved slashing blade forged in the illegal canal-side smithies of the Sump. Designed for tight quarters and quick, slashing strikes. Worn by Sump gang-lords and the runners who enforce their will.
martial_melee.flavor_text: In the Sump, personal space is a weapon.

martial_melee.name: Shortsword
martial_melee.description: Standard-issue sidearm for Citadel cadets. Functional, unadorned, deliberately boring — to remind cadets they haven't earned anything better yet. Many paint or carve their blades in protest.
martial_melee.flavor_text: The blade is plain. The ambition behind it is not.

martial_melee.name: Trident
martial_melee.description: A three-pronged spear designed to be thrown or thrust. The tines are barbed and hollow — when pulled from a wound, they whistle a distinctive Vortex death-cry.

martial_melee.name: War Pick
martial_melee.description: A piercing pick with an obsidian head and iron haft. Used by Obsidian law-enforcers to punch through armor and extract confessions. The tip is shaped like a quill — 'writing the law in blood,' as the Inquisitors say.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Every confession is a signature. The law-pick is the pen.

martial_melee.name: Warhammer
martial_melee.description: A compact warhammer engineered by Ferrum siege-smiths. The head is weighted with a core of density-enchanted iron — hits harder than its size suggests. Standard equipment for Ferrum breach teams.
martial_melee.flavor_text: Gates open. Or they don't. Either way.

martial_melee.name: Whip
martial_melee.description: A braided leather whip, the tip knotted around a jagged piece of canal-glass. Used by Sump overseers to drive laborers — and by Sump assassins to garrote a mark from the shadows.

martial_ranged.name: Blowgun
martial_ranged.description: A hollow reed tube used to launch poisoned darts. Silent, concealable, and deadly with the right toxins. The weapon of Sump assassins who prefer their targets to die quietly and days later.
martial_ranged.flavor_text: You'll feel a pinch. Then nothing. Then everything.

martial_ranged.name: Crossbow, Hand
martial_ranged.description: A palm-sized repeating crossbow of exquisite Vortex engineering. Uses a miniature wind-channel to tension the string. Small enough to conceal in a sleeve, powerful enough to pierce a noble's silk doublet.
martial_ranged.flavor_text: The wind carries death in a package no bigger than your hand.

martial_ranged.name: Crossbow, Heavy
martial_ranged.description: A reinforced heavy crossbow built to Ferrum specifications — over-engineered, heavy as a sin, and capable of punching a bolt through plate armor at 300 feet. Requires a crank to reload; requires two men to carry.
martial_ranged.flavor_text: Loud. Slow. Doesn't need to be anything else.

martial_ranged.name: Longbow
martial_ranged.description: A towering longbow crafted from the flexible heartwood of the Deep-Grove trees. The Deep-Blood clans are the only ones who know how to harvest and shape this wood — and they don't share the secret.
martial_ranged.flavor_text: The wood remembers the forest. The arrow remembers the hunt.

light.name: Padded
light.description: Layered canvas and wool padding, stiff with old sweat and canal water. Standard-issue for laborers working dangerous jobs — and for Blank conscripts who can't afford better.
light.flavor_text: It doesn't stop blades. It makes the bruises less colorful.

light.name: Leather
light.description: Cured leather armor, boiled and shaped to the wearer's body. Worn by hunters, scouts, and anyone who values mobility over protection. The Deep-Blood clans decorate theirs with tooth-and-claw trophies.
light.flavor_text: Worn leather, worn hands. Both know the hunt.

light.name: Studded Leather
light.description: Leather armor reinforced with lightweight alloy studs arranged in wind-channeling patterns. Vortex scouts swear the studs whistle warnings when arrows approach — possibly superstition, possibly Aether.
light.flavor_text: The wind tells you when to duck. Listen.

medium.name: Hide
medium.description: Raw hide armor taken from the great beasts of the outer wilds. Still smells faintly of the animal. Stone-Blood warriors believe wearing the skin transfers the beast's spirit to the wearer.
medium.flavor_text: The beast died. Its hide still fights.

medium.name: Chain Shirt
medium.description: Fine-linked chainmail shirt worn under clothing. The standard concealed armor for nobles who expect trouble at court. Ferrum weaves the links tighter than anyone else.
medium.flavor_text: Poise and posture. And steel beneath the silk.

medium.name: Scale Mail
medium.description: Armor made of overlapping metal scales, each one shaped to mimic a dragon's scale. House Ignis produces the finest — their scales are pressed from steel quenched in draconic-essence oils.
medium.flavor_text: Wear the dragon. Become the dragon.

medium.name: Breastplate
medium.description: A fitted breastplate of black-enameled steel, worn over judicial robes. The Obsidian seal is acid-etched into the chest. Worn by Archons and senior Inquisitors — it is both armor and badge of office.
medium.flavor_text: The law wears black. The law is bulletproof.

medium.name: Half Plate
medium.description: Partial plate armor covering the torso, shoulders, and thighs. Pyre-forged half-harnesses incorporate heat-dispersing channels — the wearer stays cool even as the forge-fire rages around them.

heavy.name: Ring Mail
heavy.description: Heavy rings of cheap iron sewn to a leather backing. Noisy, uncomfortable, and barely adequate. Issued to Blank conscripts who are expected to die before the armor gives out.
heavy.flavor_text: The metal rings ring like funeral bells when you walk.

heavy.name: Chain Mail
heavy.description: Full-body chainmail of Ignis manufacture. Each link is individually tempered in volcanic ash, giving the armor a dark gray sheen and superior corrosion resistance. Standard issue for professional soldiers.
heavy.flavor_text: The ash-scorched links have turned aside a thousand blades.

heavy.name: Splint
heavy.description: Interlocking vertical metal strips riveted to a chain backing. Ferrum splint armor is engineered for maximum rigidity — the wearer can brace against a charging beast and hold the line.

heavy.name: Plate
heavy.description: The finest full plate armor in Solis-Grave. Forged by Ferrum master-smiths over months, each suit fitted to a single wearer. The joints are articulated with precision engineering — a Ferrum knight can dance in this armor. A slow, heavy, terrifying dance.
heavy.flavor_text: A walking fortress. A breathing vault.

shields.name: Shield
shields.description: A large tower shield of layered steel and oak. Emblazoned with the Ferrum wall-and-anvil crest. Used in shield-wall formations that have never been broken.
shields.flavor_text: Behind this, you are not alone.

shields.name: Buckler
shields.description: A small, lightweight buckler strapped to the forearm. Vortex design incorporates wind-channeling grooves that supposedly deflect incoming projectiles — the science is dubious, but Vortex duelists swear by them.
shields.flavor_text: Small shield. Fast hands. No hits.

weapon.name: Blaze-Infused
weapon.effect: Weapon deals +1d4 fire damage on hit.
weapon.visible: Blade glows faint orange when drawn. Warm to the touch.
weapon.description: The weapon has been quenched in draconic-essence oil and tempered in an Ignis forge. The fire never fully leaves the metal.
weapon.flavor_text: It remembers the flame. It wants to share.

weapon.name: Storm-Channeled
weapon.effect: Weapon returns to your hand after being thrown. Range increases by 20 ft.
weapon.visible: Faint static crackle. Hair stands up near the blade.
weapon.description: A vortex of bound air has been sealed within the weapon. When thrown, the wind carries it back to the wielder's grip.
weapon.flavor_text: Throw it away. It'll come back. They always do.

weapon.name: Law-Bound
weapon.effect: Weapon is +1 to attack and damage against chaotic-aligned creatures. Glows blue when a lie is spoken within 10 ft.
weapon.description: The weapon has been blessed by an Obsidian Archon during a binding ceremony. It recognizes truth — and punishes chaos.
weapon.flavor_text: Lie to me. I dare you.

weapon.name: Void-Touched
weapon.effect: Weapon deals +1d6 necrotic damage. On a critical hit, the target cannot regain hit points until the start of your next turn.
weapon.visible: Blade absorbs light. Shadows pool around it.
weapon.description: The weapon has been bound to a fragment of Void essence extracted from a slain shadow-beast. It drinks light and hungers for life.
weapon.flavor_text: The darkness in the blade recognizes the darkness in you.

weapon.name: Sovereign's Edge
weapon.effect: Weapon is +2 to attack and damage. When you cast a spell through this weapon (as a focus), reduce the Spell Safety DC by 2.
weapon.visible: Blade hums with dormant draconic resonance. Dragons recognize it.
weapon.description: Forged using techniques lost since the fall of the last Sovereign dynasty. The weapon resonates with draconic blood — it knows its wielder is meant to rule.
weapon.flavor_text: This blade has crowned kings and ended dynasties.

weapon.name: God-Forged
weapon.effect: Weapon is +3 to attack and damage. Ignores all damage resistances. On a hit, target must make a DC 18 CON save or take an additional 3d6 elemental damage of the wielder's draconic affinity.
weapon.visible: Blade exists partially in the Aether. Phases through non-living matter like mist when not in combat.
weapon.description: A weapon that bridges the material and Aether realms. Not truly physical — it exists wherever it needs to be. Only those with near-perfect blood purity can even perceive its true form.
weapon.flavor_text: Priceless. Cannot be bought. Must be earned through blood, sacrifice, and the favor of gods who no longer answer prayers.

armor.name: Aether-Warded
armor.effect: Reduce Aether Burn damage taken by 1d6. Once per long rest, reroll a failed Spell Safety save.
armor.description: The armor has been treated with Aether-dampening compounds that bleed off excess magical energy before it reaches the wearer's skin.
armor.flavor_text: The Aether burns. This armor gives you a second chance to pull back.

armor.name: Dragon-Scaled
armor.effect: Armor grants +1 AC. Gain resistance to one elemental damage type (chosen at forging).
armor.description: Genuine dragon scales have been fused into the armor's surface. The scales retain a fragment of the dragon's elemental nature.
armor.flavor_text: The dragon's protection, worn by mortal shoulders.

armor.name: Sovereign Mantle
armor.effect: Armor is +2 AC. When you are reduced to 0 HP, you instead drop to 1 HP. 1/long rest.
armor.description: Woven with threads of pure draconic essence. The armor refuses to let its wearer fall — a Sovereign does not die easily.
armor.flavor_text: You will not fall today. The armor has decided.

armor.name: God-Shroud
armor.effect: Armor is +3 AC. You are immune to Aether Burn while wearing this armor. Once per long rest, you can cast a 5th-level spell without any Spell Safety check.
armor.description: Armor that exists between realms — a shroud woven from the fabric of the Aether itself. The wearer walks untouched by the forces that consume lesser mages.
armor.flavor_text: The Aether does not burn gods. In this armor, neither does it burn you.

aether_cores.name: Fractured Aether-Core
aether_cores.description: A cracked, leaking sphere of crystallized Aether essence. Pulled from the corpse of a lesser monster — barely stable, but enough to power a cantrip or coat a blade for one glorious strike.

aether_cores.name: Intact Aether-Core
aether_cores.description: A complete, stable Aether-Core harvested from a mid-tier monster. Glows with a steady inner light. Prized by spellcasters and alchemists alike.
aether_cores.flavor_text: It pulses like a second heart in your palm.

aether_cores.name: Pristine Aether-Core
aether_cores.description: A flawless core from a powerful creature. The essence inside is concentrated, pure, and dangerously potent. Enchanters pay fortunes for these.
aether_cores.flavor_text: Perfect. Untouched. Worth more than you are.

aether_cores.name: Sovereign-Fragment
aether_cores.description: A shard of something that was once godlike. The essence within is barely contained — holding it feels like holding a star. Only the most powerful spellcasters can safely use these.
aether_cores.flavor_text: This is not a core. This is a piece of something that should not have died.

adventuring_gear.name: Blood Crystal Testing Kit
adventuring_gear.description: A portable kit containing a reactive blood crystal and a lancet. Prick the finger, place a drop of blood on the crystal, and watch the color change to determine blood purity percentage. Single-use. Accuracy: 95%.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: Legal only with an Obsidian permit. Most users don't have one.

adventuring_gear.name: Aether Purifier (Potion)
adventuring_gear.description: A bitter potion that neutralizes Aether Burn damage already suffered. Drinking it feels like swallowing liquid copper and regret — but it removes 2d4+2 points of Aether Burn.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: Tastes like copper. Tastes like regret. Tastes like survival.

adventuring_gear.name: Core Injector Kit
adventuring_gear.description: A spring-loaded device that crushes an Aether-Core and sprays the essence onto a weapon's striking surface. Applies a core coating as a bonus action. 3 uses before requiring a new core.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: Pull the trigger. Coat the blade. Make it count.

adventuring_gear.name: Dragon-Bone Charm
adventuring_gear.description: A small charm carved from fossilized dragon bone, strung on a leather cord. When worn, grants advantage on one Spell Safety saving throw per long rest. The charm crumbles to dust after use.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: The bone remembers how to resist the Aether. Once.

adventuring_gear.name: Inquisitor's Seal
adventuring_gear.description: A wax-sealed writ bearing the Obsidian Inquisitorial stamp. Allows the bearer to legally cast spells within Obsidian territory. Forged seals are indistinguishable from real ones and cost the same on the black market.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: A piece of paper that stands between a mage and the pyre.

adventuring_gear.name: Cult Whispers (Book)
adventuring_gear.description: A hand-printed collection of forbidden prophecy fragments circulated by the Cult of the Sixth. Possession is punishable by immediate Inquisitorial Smite — no trial, no appeal.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: Read it. Memorize it. Burn it. Pray you weren't seen.

adventuring_gear.name: Citadel Cadet Uniform
adventuring_gear.description: The gray-and-black uniform of a Citadel academy cadet. Identifies the wearer as a recruit. Reduces hazing from instructors (slightly). Increases hazing from senior cadets (significantly).
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: Wear it with pride. Or don't. They'll fix that attitude.

adventuring_gear.name: Sump-Ration
adventuring_gear.description: A day's sustenance wrapped in waxed paper: dried fungus strips, hard black bread, salted canal-fish. Provides enough nutrition to keep a laborer standing for one day. Tastes worse than it smells.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: If you can afford better, you don't eat this.

adventuring_gear.name: Pyre Lantern
adventuring_gear.description: A small alchemical lantern containing a captured forge-flame in a glass housing. Produces 30 ft. bright light and 60 ft. dim light. Burns indefinitely without fuel — the flame is self-sustaining through alchemical reaction.
adventuring_gear.flavor_text: A piece of Pyre's eternal fire, caged in glass.

tools_kits.name: Alchemist's Supplies
tools_kits.description: Glass vials, copper tubing, mortar and pestle, and a burner assembly. Used to refine Aether-Cores, brew potions, and identify unknown substances.
tools_kits.flavor_text: Science. Magic. Same thing, different labels.

tools_kits.name: Smith's Tools
tools_kits.description: Hammer, tongs, chisel, and a small portable anvil. The hallmark of a traveling smith. Used to repair weapons, sharpen blades, and forge simple equipment.
tools_kits.flavor_text: The fire is your temple. The anvil is your altar.

tools_kits.name: Thieves' Tools
tools_kits.description: A roll of lockpicks, tension wrenches, and a small mirror on a telescoping rod. Essential equipment for anyone who needs to go where they're not welcome.
tools_kits.flavor_text: Every lock is a question. These are the answers.

tools_kits.name: Herbalism Kit
tools_kits.description: Dried herbs, bandages, a small knife, and pouches for gathering. Used to identify healing plants, create poultices, and brew basic antidotes.
tools_kits.flavor_text: The forest provides. If you know what to look for.

tools_kits.name: Poisoner's Kit
tools_kits.description: Vials, syringes, and a selection of base toxins. Used to extract poisons from creatures, brew lethal concoctions, and apply venom to weapons.
tools_kits.flavor_text: Everything is poison. The dose makes the cure — or the kill.

tools_kits.name: Navigator's Tools (Star-Chart)
tools_kits.description: A star chart printed on treated silk, a sextant, and a compass. Used by Vortex sky-scouts to navigate by the constellations when flying above cloud cover.
tools_kits.flavor_text: The stars don't lie. The wind might.

tools_kits.name: Forgery Kit (Seal-Press)
tools_kits.description: Wax blocks, a small heat source, and a collection of blank seal stamps. Used to create convincing duplicates of official House and Church documents.
tools_kits.flavor_text: The seal is real. The authority behind it is not.

mounts_vehicles.name: Draft Horse
mounts_vehicles.description: A sturdy, unremarkable draft horse. Common across Solis-Grave as a beast of burden. Notable for its complete lack of self-preservation instinct — it will stand placidly while buildings burn around it.
mounts_vehicles.flavor_text: Sturdy. Stubborn. Has seen worse than you.

mounts_vehicles.name: Riding Horse
mounts_vehicles.description: A standard riding horse, bred for speed and temperament. The default mount for merchants, messengers, and adventurers who haven't earned anything grander.
mounts_vehicles.flavor_text: Four legs, a saddle, and the open road.

mounts_vehicles.name: Warhorse
mounts_vehicles.description: A massive warhorse bred by Ferrum for heavy cavalry. Trained to charge into spears, trample infantry, and fight independently when the rider falls. Has one weakness: a WIS save is required to keep it from panicking when Sovereign-level Aether is unleashed nearby.
mounts_vehicles.flavor_text: Hooves like thunder. Heart like iron. Terrified of dragons.

mounts_vehicles.name: Hippogriff
mounts_vehicles.description: A trained hippogriff from the Vortex sky-aeries. Capable of flight at twice its ground speed. Owning one requires a House Vortex license — unlicensed ownership is punishable by execution, no exceptions.
mounts_vehicles.flavor_text: The sky belongs to Vortex. Don't forget it.

mounts_vehicles.name: Wyvern
mounts_vehicles.description: A wyvern captured and broken to saddle. Only characters with Sovereign-level blood purity (50%+) can attempt to tame one — it requires a DC 18 WIS save. Wyverns are not sold. They are taken, through violence or impossible trust.
mounts_vehicles.flavor_text: This is not a mount. This is a declaration of war with wings.
